installation_steps.description: Physically connect the ADS7846 touchscreen to the Raspberry Pi GPIO header and ensure it is powered.
Check if the touchscreen is detected as an input device.
Ensure the X server is active to support graphical input.
Identify the touchscreen device ID (usually listed as ADS7846 Touchscreen).

content: Option  "InvertY"       "1"      # Flip Y-axis to correct direction
installation_steps.description: This configuration ensures correct axis mapping for the touchscreen.
Apply the new calibration settings by restarting the X server.
Prevent accidental overwrites of the calibration file.

troubleshooting.solution: Set InvertX to 1 in the calibration config.
Set InvertY to 1 in the calibration config.
Try setting SwapXY to 1 and adjust InvertX/InvertY accordingly.
Ensure X11 is running and the file is located in /etc/X11/xorg.conf.d/.

notes: Do not run LCD35-show or similar scripts as they may overwrite calibration settings.
Always test touch input after restarting X server to confirm changes.
